title: Confirmed World Cup Lineups: Colombia vs. DR Congo

@eskeFussballTue 20:36June 23, 2026 at 20:36
World Cup: Luis Díaz starts as Colombia eye Round of 32 with DR Congo test
FC Bayern’s superstar is in Colombia’s starting lineup for the game against DR Congo.
David Ramos/Getty Images
Luis Díaz starts for Colombia in their second group stage match at the 2026 World Cup against DR Congo. FC Bayern’s superstar won “Man of the Match” against Uzbekistan after contributing a goal and an assist in the 3-1 victory.
Former Bayern attacker James Rodriguez also keeps his place in Néstor Lorenzo's unchanged starting XI, while three more players with past Bundesliga experience start on the bench for Los Cafeteros – Santiago Arias, Gustavo Puerta (both Bayer Leverkusen), and Jhon Córdoba (Mainz, Köln and Hertha BSC).
Yoane Wissa helped DR Congo to their first-ever World Cup point when they held Portugal on matchday one. Cristiano Ronaldo’s historic night in Houston lifted five-star Portugal as they sit top of the group with four points.
Here are the two lineups for the game at Guadalajara Stadium.
Colombia: Vargas; Muñoz, Lucumí, Sánchez, Mojica; Lerma, Puerta; James Rodríguez ©, Luis Díaz, Arias; Luis Suárez
DR Congo: Mpasi; Wan-Bissaka, Kapuadi, Mbemba ©, Tuanzebe, Masuaku; Moutoussamy, Kayembe, Mukau; Wissa, Bakambu
